matches.rationale: Same Bash commit action and the evidence explicitly requires logical commit units in Conventional Commit format.
Same Bash PR-creation action; the evidence specifies creating the PR from the current branch into main.
The rule evidence explicitly requires checking status with gh run watch after launching the Release workflow.
Same Bash merge action, with evidence exactly prohibiting an immediate post-creation merge and requiring a CI check first.
Same manual version-edit target and same Edit tool: package.json is the file to edit. The extraction additionally mentions the synchronization follow-up, but its Edit trigger covers the gold action.

misses.note: No extracted Bash rule targets waiting for CI with gh pr checks --watch.
No unused extracted rule requires squash-merging the release PR after CI passes; the sole merge extraction is closest to and consumed by g11.
No extracted rule targets launching Release with the requested patch/minor/major bump; the workflow-related extraction instead expresses the later status check.
No unused extracted rule specifically prohibits merging when CI fails; the sole merge extraction is closest to and consumed by g11.
Compiler-reported skip: "이미 main 브랜치에 있고 변경사항이 없으면 릴리즈만 실행 — 조건부 워크플로우 분기 로직으로 단일 tool 트리거로 표현 불가".
The required PR-to-CI-to-merge procedure, including the manual-cancel alternative, was not extracted as its own rule; the single merge rule is closest to g11 and cannot be reused.
No extracted Bash rule targets the specifically prohibited one-line gh pr create && gh pr merge chain.
Compiler-reported skip: "Release 트리거는 workflow_dispatch만 사용되고 main push로는 릴리스되지 않는다는 설명 — 시스템 동작 설명일 뿐 에이전트 행동 규칙이 아님".
The document implies that a workflow-driven release should not involve manual local version edits, but no extracted Edit rule binds that prohibition.
The extracted package.json rule is Edit-only and is consumed by g14; no Bash rule captures the referenced follow-up node scripts/sync-version.mjs command.
The recommendation to choose workflow-based automatic version management was not extracted as a Bash action rule.
